fingerprint: Section 6.4 correctly identifies the relabeling pattern but underexploits two devastating quantitative findings: (1) monitor.py source code confirms zero dome-parameter involvement in the 'predictions' — tidal M2 is lunar_circuit/2 (line 442), λ_A is literally c/70.0 (line 576), P-wave shadow is qualitative 'Shadow exists' (line 605); (2) the derives_from_dome code_analysis tag is false for ALL 49 relabeling WINs — 100% parasitic on standard physics values, zero independent dome derivations.
globe_value: Standard physics constants (M2=24.84/2, H₀=70, Schumann=c/2πR, etc.) imported directly into dome monitoring code
dome_value: Same numerical values relabeled as 'aetheric resonance,' 'aetheric redshift,' 'sub-terrestrial wall'
match_precision: 100% — dome imports globe values with no modification
why_it_matters: The dome's monitoring code proves the section's central claim computationally: disc_radius, firmament_height, and sun_altitude appear nowhere in the calculation of any relabeled prediction. The predicted values would be numerically identical with or without a dome framework. Additionally, 0/49 relabeling WINs have derives_from_dome=true in code_analysis tags — this is the missing quantitative anchor the section needs.

key_findings.significance: M2 tidal period computed from lunar circuit / 2 — identical to standard formula. No dome parameter involved.
Aetheric redshift scale is literally c / H₀ with H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc hardcoded. Standard cosmology's Hubble constant imported as a literal.
P-wave shadow zone 'prediction' is a qualitative string, not computed from dome geometry. The dome doesn't even attempt to derive the 104°-140° range from its own parameters.

NMP drift rate uses globe-calibrated coordinate math: 111.0 km/deg and cos(lat) correction are spherical Earth formulas. On the dome's own disc, equatorial longitude conversion should be 174.7 km/deg, not 111.0 — a 57% discrepancy that vanishes only near the pole where the azimuthal equidistant projection converges to the globe.

code_search.dome_parameters_absent: disc_radius (20015), firmament_height (9086), sun_altitude (5733) do not appear in any of the relabeling WIN calculations in monitor.py

suggested_text: The dome's own monitoring code confirms this quantitatively: of 49 WINs that relabel standard physics, none — zero — derive their predicted values from dome geometry (disc radius, firmament height, or sun altitude). The code computes M2 as lunar_circuit/2, λ_A as c/70.0, and checks the P-wave shadow zone as a qualitative 'shadow exists.' Remove the dome labels and the predictions are numerically unchanged. The dome framework is not just redundant with standard physics — it is computationally absent from its own predictions.
